channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Microsoft Warns: Fake Perplexity Extension Abused Chrome Search Features
item.description: <p>Microsoft found a fake Perplexity AI Chrome extension that rerouted searches through attacker servers. Here’s what users should check now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-perplexity-chrome-extension-searches/">Microsoft Warns: Fake Perplexity Extension Abused Chrome Search Features</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
item.description: Attackers fingerprint victims through user-agent data to deliver OS-specific payloads, increasing compromise rates and campaign profitability.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
item.description: Researchers say the highly effective social engineering technique is no longer the exception for malware attacks — it's now the rule.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
item.description:
Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the component's internal network port.
Synacktiv, which found the bug, says it can lead to a full cluster takeover. There is no fix and no CVE. The firm says it reported the flaw to Argo CD's maintainers in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
item.description:
A teenager accused of belonging to the hacking group Scattered Spider has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud, the U.S. Department of Justice&nbsp;announced&nbsp;on July 1.
Peter Stokes, 19, a dual U.S. and Estonian citizen, appeared in a Chicago federal court on June 30, where a judge ordered him held in custody.
Finnish police
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Aflac Data Breach: Over 4M Customers in Japan May Be at Risk
item.description: <p>Aflac says a data breach in Japan may affect 4.38 million customers and agents, exposing personal, policy, and some banking information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-aflac-japan-data-breach-insurance-records/">Aflac Data Breach: Over 4M Customers in Japan May Be at Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Microsoft Uncovers Widespread Hotel Phishing Campaign in Japan
item.description: <p>Microsoft and Trend Micro found hotel phishing attacks using fake guest complaints and photo links to target staff in Japan.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-hotel-phishing-apac-japan/">Microsoft Uncovers Widespread Hotel Phishing Campaign in Japan</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Microsoft Adds New Teams Controls to Block Unauthorized AI Bots From Meetings
item.description: <p>Microsoft's new Teams admin policy requires organizer approval for external AI bots, giving organizations greater visibility and control over automated participants in sensitive meetings.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-adds-new-teams-controls-to-block-unauthorized-ai-bots-from-meetings/">Microsoft Adds New Teams Controls to Block Unauthorized AI Bots From Meetings</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: SEO-Poisoned Software Sites Abuse ScreenConnect to Deploy AsyncRAT
item.description:
Unknown threat actors are leveraging the ScreenConnect remote access tool as a way to deploy and execute AsyncRAT.
Kaspersky said the activity is part of a "massive, multi-domain, multi-language" campaign that distributes malicious installer archives hosted on spoofed websites.
These installers masquerade as popular software like OBS Studio, DNS Jumper, DS4Windows, and Bandicam, among others.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new multi-stage malware delivery attack chain that uses social engineering and Blogger pages to deliver an information stealer called PureLogs.
The activity has been codenamed VEIL#DROP by Securonix. It's suspected that the initial payloads are distributed either via spear-phishing or a drive-by compromise, which occurs when an unsuspecting user lands on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Shadow IT: Tiering the Unseen to Manage Vendor Risk | UpGuard
item.description: Invisible apps become riskier with time. Use real-time user signals to categorize every vendor in your ecosystem based on its true risk profile.

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: Six MCP Security Incidents Every Security Leader Should Know | UpGuard
item.description: From registry poisoning to filesystem wipes: discover the 6 MCP security incidents every leader must know to secure their AI agent workflows in 2026.

item.title: Shadow MCP Servers: The AI Infrastructure You Can't See | UpGuard
item.description: In 2012, it was Dropbox. In 2026, it’s Shadow MCP. Discover why unvetted AI agents are an invisible threat and how to regain total visibility.

item.title: Practical MCP Security: A Playbook for Mid-Market Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Move from zero to a defensible MCP security baseline in 90 days. Get a practical, phased playbook for lean teams to secure agentic AI infrastructure.

item.title: Ousaban Banking Trojan Targets Iberian Bank Users with Fake PDF Lures
item.description:
A Brazilian banking trojan called Ousaban is going after Windows users who bank in Spain and Portugal.&nbsp;Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs&nbsp;identified the campaign in May 2026.
It opens with a phishing PDF disguised as a corrupted file, checks that the visitor is really in Spain or Portugal, and hides its real payload inside an image.
The goal is the usual one: steal banking logins and take
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Adobe Patches 7 CVSS 10.0 Flaws in ColdFusion and Campaign Classic
item.description:
Adobe has released patches for multiple maximum-severity security flaws impacting Adobe ColdFusion and Adobe Campaign Classic.
The ColdFusion updates "resolves critical and important vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, arbitrary file system read, and security feature bypass," Adobe said in an alert released Tuesday.
The vulnerabilities are listed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat
item.description: LLMs consistently hallucinate Web domains for legitimate brands that attackers can register for malicious activity in a difficult-to-detect attack vector.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Critical Cursor Flaws Could Let Prompt Injection Escape Sandbox and Run Commands
item.description:
Two flaws in Cursor, an AI code editor, could let a single, ordinary-looking prompt break out of the editor's safety sandbox and run any command on a developer's computer. There is no click to fall for and no approval box to ignore.
Cato AI Labs&nbsp;found the pair and named them DuneSlide. They are tracked as&nbsp;CVE-2026-50548&nbsp;and&nbsp;CVE-2026-50549, both rated 9.8 out of 10 (or 9.3
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fileless Malware Abuses Google Blogspot to Deploy Infostealer in Memory
item.description: Securonix said the Veil#Drop campaign abuses Google Blogspot to deliver PureLog Stealer in memory

item.title: Free Windows 10 Security Updates Run Until 2027, but Not Every PC Qualifies
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s consumer ESU program includes a no-cost enrollment option for eligible Windows 10 PCs, but managed devices and some account setups are excluded.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-10-esu-eligibility/">Free Windows 10 Security Updates Run Until 2027, but Not Every PC Qualifies</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Pre-Auth RCE Flaw Faces Active Exploitation Attempts
item.description:
A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Progress Kemp LoadMaster is seeing active exploitation attempts, according to an advisory from eSentire's Threat Response Unit (TRU).
The Canadian cybersecurity company said it identified exploitation attempts targeting CVE-2026-8037 (CVSS score: 9.6), an operating system (OS) command injection flaw that could be exploited to achieve
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Brazilian Banking Trojan Ousaban Targets Spain and Portugal
item.description: FortiGuard says the Brazilian banking trojan Ousaban is targeting Spain and Portugal via phishing

item.title: Safe Events Start With Threat Intel and Digital Security
item.description: Planning ahead to defend against cyber threats is the work that keeps events uneventful.
item.dc:creator: Olga Polishchuk

item.title: AI-Generated Browser Ransomware Abuses Chromium API on Windows and Android
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware artifact generated using DeepSeek that constructed a novel attack path combining "unrealistic browser-malware concepts with a real browser capability" to turn it into a working ransomware technique that runs entirely inside the browser on both Windows and Android devices.
"This is the first documented case where a frontier AI model
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Edge AI History Search Raises New Privacy Questions for IT Teams
item.description: <p>Microsoft Edge 150 keeps AI history search policy in play, giving IT teams a reason to audit browser AI controls, Copilot access, search suggestions, and extensions.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-edge-ai-history-privacy/">Microsoft Edge AI History Search Raises New Privacy Questions for IT Teams</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
item.description:
Organizations have never had greater awareness of cyber risk. Yet turning that awareness into operational resilience has never been more challenging. The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment confirms this is the case, as this year's findings reveal a series of surprising contradictions.
Here are a few examples, based on the independent survey of 1,200 IT and cybersecurity professionals
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion, Campaign Classic Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Seven of the security defects have a maximum severity rating of 10/10 and could lead to arbitrary code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/adobe-patches-critical-coldfusion-campaign-classic-vulnerabilities/">Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion, Campaign Classic Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Citrix Patches NetScaler Vulnerabilities, Including New ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Attack
item.description: <p>Citrix urges customers to patch NetScaler after fixing six vulnerabilities, including the HTTP/2 Bomb flaw and a high-severity CitrixBleed-style information disclosure bug.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/citrix-patches-netscaler-vulnerabilities-including-new-http-2-bomb-attack/">Citrix Patches NetScaler Vulnerabilities, Including New ‘HTTP/2 Bomb’ Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Are Back with New Security Guardrails
item.description: The new classifier in Fable 5 blocks the jailbreak technique that prompted the US export controls “in over 99% of cases”

item.title: Papa Johns Surveillance-Based Advertising
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday said it's accelerating its quantum safe security roadmap, stating technology advances in quantum computing are making it essential to replace existing encryption standards sooner than previously expected.
"Advances in quantum research and development have shifted the risk horizon," Mark Russinovich, chief technology officer of Microsoft Azure, said. "We believe
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Accelerates Quantum-Safe Push with New Timeline
item.description: Microsoft has brought forward its timelines for transitioning to post-quantum cryptography (PQC)

item.title: Frontier AI: Six Questions Every Enterprise Should Ask Security Vendors
item.description: <p>From model selection and automation to validation and measurable results, the right questions can help enterprises separate genuine AI capabilities from marketing hype.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/frontier-ai-six-questions-every-enterprise-should-ask-security-vendors/">Frontier AI: Six Questions Every Enterprise Should Ask Security Vendors</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joshua Goldfarb

item.title: Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities Across iOS, macOS, and Safari
item.description: <p>The updates fix vulnerabilities in WebKit, the kernel, WebRTC, Web Extensions, and other components affecting iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Safari users.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/apple-patches-dozens-of-vulnerabilities-across-ios-macos-and-safari/">Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities Across iOS, macOS, and Safari</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Insurance Giant Aflac Discloses Data Breach Impacting Millions
item.description: Aflac Japan has notified regulators that policy details and personal and banking information have been compromised

item.title: Dawnguard Raises $6.3 Million for Security Architecture Automation Platform
item.description: <p>The company has publicly launched its solution to help organizations design, build, and operate secure cloud systems.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/dawnguard-raises-6-3-million-for-security-architecture-automation-platform/">Dawnguard Raises $6.3 Million for Security Architecture Automation Platform</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI
item.description: <p>Hackers were seen making over 81 million login attempts originating from systems associated with hosting provider LSHIY.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/massive-password-spray-campaign-targeting-azure-cli/">Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Phantom Squatting Uses AI-Hallucinated Domains for Phishing and Malware
item.description:
Large language models keep inventing web addresses that do not exist. Attackers have started buying those made-up domains before anyone else can, then hosting phishing pages on them to catch traffic that AI tools point their way.
Palo Alto Networks'&nbsp;Unit 42&nbsp;calls the trick phantom squatting, and its new research shows it is already happening in the wild.
The reason it matters is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic Restores Claude Fable 5 After U.S. Lifts Jailbreak-Linked Export Controls
item.description:
Anthropic is putting Claude Fable 5 back online worldwide. On&nbsp;June 30, the U.S. Commerce Department lifted the export controls it had imposed on Fable and its more tightly controlled sibling Mythos 5 about two and a half weeks earlier.
Fable 5 returns to users on Wednesday, July 1, across Claude.ai, the Claude Platform, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork.
Export controls restrict who can
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>Fifteen of the newly patched flaws have been rated ‘critical’ and 67 have been rated ‘high severity’.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/google-patches-382-chrome-vulnerabilities/">Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "massive, ongoing, automated password spray attack" aimed at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface (CLI), compromising dozens of accounts in the process.
The activity, per Huntress, originates from an IPv6 address range (2a0a:d683::/32) controlled by internet infrastructure provider LSHIY LLC (AS32167).
"Between June 12 and June 26, the threat
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researcher Analyzes 3,000 Live ClickFix Payloads, Exposing API-Driven Malware Delivery
item.description:
ClickFix, the trick that fools people into running malware by hand, has quietly grown a back office.
New research shows the malicious commands behind its fake "prove you're human" pages are now handed out by API-driven servers that give each visitor the same malware in a different disguise. The same research also turned up a new delivery method built to slip past Windows' script scanning.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Citrix Patches Six NetScaler Flaws Allowing File Read and Denial-of-Service
item.description:
Citrix on Tuesday released security updates to address multiple flaws in NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) that could be exploited by an attacker to facilitate arbitrary file reads or trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-8451 (CVSS score: 8.8) - An insufficient input validation
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems
item.description: The group compromised at least 10 regional organizations, including two state-owned entities, and deployed a new backdoor.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
item.description: &quot;Agentjacking&quot; is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Attackers Seize Exposed AI Endpoints to Power Offensive Ops
item.description: Threat actors don't need any special authentication to reach a target endpoint — they just need to know where it is.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Why Identity Security Is Your Cyber Career Entry Point
item.description: As AI reshapes cybersecurity workflows, John Paul Cunningham, CISO at Silverfort, says the technology is creating opportunities rather than eliminating jobs — and there are more ways than ever to break into the essential field.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Beek

item.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
item.description: Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
item.description:
New Microsoft&nbsp;research&nbsp;shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to an outsider.
The trick is that the agent never breaks a rule. Every step looks routine, so in a default setup no alarm may fire.
The work comes from Microsoft Incident Response and its
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
item.description:
A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and online services offline.
Researchers at QiAnXin's&nbsp;XLab&nbsp;have tracked it since February 2026, and say the real story is not how big it is today, but how fast it is changing.
The end goal is a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Nissan Discloses Employee Data Breach Linked to Oracle Zero-Day
item.description: Nissan says employees' data was stolen via the Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day campaign

item.title: Bring PCI SSC Training to Your Organization with the New Training Venue Host Program
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
item.description:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner.
The activity has been found to weaponize CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS score: 9.3), an unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Langflow, indicating threat actors are scanning and targeting exposed artificial intelligence (AI)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an active browser extension campaign that is designed to steal cryptocurrency by stealthily replacing wallet addresses when unsuspecting users initiate a transaction.
The cryptocurrency clipper activity has been codenamed Silent Swap by McAfee Labs.
"The campaign is delivered through unsigned installers – observed in both .NET and Golang variants – that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Meta Adds WhatsApp Usernames: Here’s What You Need to Know
item.description: <p>WhatsApp is rolling out usernames so people can chat without sharing phone numbers. Here’s how reservations, username keys, and rules work.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-usernames-phone-number-privacy/">Meta Adds WhatsApp Usernames: Here&#8217;s What You Need to Know</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited For Malware Delivery
item.description: Attackers exploited a critical SimpleHelp RMM bug to deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer malware

item.title: This month in security with Tony Anscombe – June 2026 edition
item.description: Three-day patching deadlines, exposed fuel-tank systems, scams costing billions of dollars, and social media bans for children all gave Tony plenty to unpack in June 2026

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.
New research from&nbsp;Adversa AI, which is named the bypass GuardFall, found it works against ten of the eleven popular open-source coding and computer-use agents the firm tested. Only one, "Continue," was built to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SimpleHelp Flaw Exploited to Deploy Malware Targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description: <p>A SimpleHelp authentication flaw is being exploited to deploy Djinn Stealer, a cross-platform malware targeting cloud, developer, and AI credentials.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-simplehelp-flaw-djinn-stealer-developer-credentials/">SimpleHelp Flaw Exploited to Deploy Malware Targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: iPhone Security Fixes May Arrive Sooner as AI Speeds Up Threats
item.description: <p>Apple is releasing some iPhone security fixes earlier as AI raises concerns about faster cyberattacks and shorter patch windows. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-iphone-ai-security-updates/">iPhone Security Fixes May Arrive Sooner as AI Speeds Up Threats</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: <p>The Microsoft Defender vulnerability CVE-2026-33825 was exploited in the wild as a zero-day before patches were released.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/bluehammer-vulnerability-exploited-in-ransomware-attacks/">BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.
In many cases, the path in was visible just by watching what the app sent: a plaintext API key, a reusable token, or a backend server that accepted requests with no key at all.
Whoever grabs it can send model requests on the developer's account,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: iPhone 18 Leak: Apple’s Next Pro Design May Have Appeared Online
item.description: <p>Leaked Tata files reportedly show possible iPhone 18 Pro design details, factory images, and supplier records ahead of Apple’s expected September launch.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-iphone-18-pro-design-leak-tata-breach/">iPhone 18 Leak: Apple’s Next Pro Design May Have Appeared Online</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: AI-Generated Workflows Are a Silent Security Disaster
item.description: Teams are dealing with a truly dangerous problem — automation that works, but that no one understands.
item.dc:creator: Yelena Mujibur Sheikh

item.title: Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: <p>Decades-old Bash shell tricks can bypass safeguards in most open source AI coding agents, potentially turning malicious repositories into supply chain attack vectors.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/decades-old-bash-tricks-expose-ai-coding-agents-to-supply-chain-attacks/">Decades-Old Bash Tricks Expose AI Coding Agents to Supply Chain Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: The Realities of AI Video Surveillance
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: ClickFix Now Cybercriminals' Favorite Malware Delivery Technique
item.description: ReliaQuest report warns of a surge in ClickFix social engineering attacks against Windows and macOS users

item.title: Verifiable Digital Credential Presentment
item.description: This blog post is #4 in our series on Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDCs). Our other posts can be found via Post #1, Post #2, and Post #3. In earlier posts, we discussed how verifiable digital credentials (VDCs) are issued and compared the underlying credential formats (ISO/IEC “mdoc” vs. W3C Verifiable Credentials). In this post, we turn to the other side of the story: presentation; that is, how a holder shows their VDC to a verifier at runtime in both in-person and online contexts. We’ll again explore the mobile driver’s license (mDL) use case, consider the differences between ISO/IEC
item.dc:creator: Bill Fisher, Ryan Galluzzo, Heather Flanagan

item.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
item.description:
The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed. Threat actor activity was pre-planned, months out, across three sectors and at least ten languages.
Check Point Exposure Management published the FIFA World Cup 2026 Cyber Threat Report this month, covering
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
item.description:
An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer.
The intrusion involves the exploitation of CVE-2026-48558 (CVSS score: 10.0), a critical authentication bypass vulnerability impacting the OpenID Connect (OIDC) flow that an unauthenticated
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Leverage Blockchain to Hit Japan's Hotels Through Booking.com Phishing
item.description: A wave of phishing emails sent to Booking.com partner accommodations in Japan in May led to blockchain-hosted malware

item.title: UK Healthcare Sector Records Tenfold Increase in Cyber-Attacks
item.description: SonicWall records 264,000 events in first five months of 2026 as UK hospitals come under siege

item.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
item.description:
Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network.
An attacker within wireless range, with just a laptop and no prior connection, can crash the sharing service on a Mac or iPhone set to receive from anyone, with no tap or prompt.
The same research found Quick Share flaws that
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Best Data Breach & Data Leak Detection Tools | UpGuard
item.description: Stopping an intrusion is half the battle. This guide ranks the top breach and leak detection tools to neutralize exposed credentials before attackers do.

item.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
item.description:
Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind&nbsp;BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six AI browsers and assistants into copying a user's credentials and sending them to an attacker.
The targets included OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension.
An
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over 300 UK Firms Hit by Ransomware in a Year
item.description: Report Fraud data reveals that more than half of 323 UK ransomware victims last year were SMEs

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
item.description:
A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API.
The flaw, tracked as&nbsp;CVE-2026-8037, carries a CVSS score of&nbsp;9.8 according to ZDI. A patch is available. If you run LoadMaster with the API enabled, update now.
Progress&nbsp;published its advisory on June
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
item.description:
A critical security flaw impacting Oracle E-Business Suite has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to Defused Cyber.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS score: 9.8), refers to an improper privilege management and authentication flaw in Oracle Payments that could be abused to take over susceptible instances.
"Easily exploitable vulnerability allows
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What Is Sales Enablement? A Guide for Security and Compliance Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Discover why security reviews are the hidden sales enablement bottleneck, and learn a strategy for overcoming this hurdle to accelerate deal velocity.

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools and Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the top vulnerability management software (VMS) platforms of 2026. This comprehensive guide reviews industry leaders like UpGuard, Tenable, Qualys,

item.title: Top 25 Cyberattacks in Sports: Does Defense Win Championships? | UpGuard
item.description: From malicious insider espionage to devastating double-extortion campaigns, here are the top 25 cyberattacks in sports history.

item.title: What Is an RFP Response? A Guide for Security and GRC Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Stop letting the RFP security section bottleneck your sales. Learn how GRC teams can streamline response workflows, map controls, and close deals faster

item.title: Implementing A Vendor Risk Assessment Process in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: A framework for implementing an efficient vendor risk assessment workflow into a Vendor Risk Management program in 2026.

item.title: Vendor Due Diligence Questionnaire (Free Template) | UpGuard
item.description: Learn all about vendor due diligence questionnaires, and customize one for your organization using our free VDDQ template.

item.title: Vendor Risk Assessments: An Ultimate Guide | UpGuard
item.description: Master vendor risk assessment: frameworks, real examples, and tools. Learn how automation and continuous monitoring strengthen your security.

item.title: How to Answer a Security Questionnaire: A 4-Step Guide | UpGuard
item.description: Learn best practices on how to answer a security questionnaire as a vendor.

item.title: What is a Security Questionnaire? | UpGuard
item.description: A security questionnaire helps organizations identify potential cybersecurity weaknesses among their third-party and even fourth-party vendors. Learn more.

item.title: Vendor Risk Management Checklist (Updated 2026) | UpGuard
item.description: This is a complete vendor risk management checklist. Learn how to reduce your third and fourth-party risk with this in-depth post.

item.title: NIST Enrichment Reductions Impact CVE Coverage, Accuracy
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) scaled back the number of CVEs it selects for in-depth analysis, but the move has produced mixed results, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: DOJ Seizes 400 Illegal FIFA World Cup Streaming Domains
item.description: <p>Officials from the US Department of Justice seized nearly 400 domains linked to illegal World Cup streams and warned viewers about the risks of malware, phishing, and data theft.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-doj-illegal-world-cup-streaming-domains/">DOJ Seizes 400 Illegal FIFA World Cup Streaming Domains</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Japanese Telecom Giant Says Breach May Expose 14.2 Million Email Accounts
item.description: <p>KDDI says a breach may have exposed email addresses and passwords for up to 14.2 million ISP accounts across six providers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-kddi-breach-isp-email-accounts-apac-japan/">Japanese Telecom Giant Says Breach May Expose 14.2 Million Email Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
item.description: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
item.description: One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Can Clothes Make You Invisible to Facial Recognition?
item.description: Does life feel Orwellian sometimes? One researcher has a solution for you: graphic tees that confuse the neural networks in surveillance cameras.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
item.description: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: AI-Driven Identity Attacks Are Surging, PwC Warns
item.description: <p>AI has given cybercriminals a big advantage in attacking organizations, which they are using to go after weaknesses on edge devices</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-pwc-ai-identity-edge-device-cyber-threats/">AI-Driven Identity Attacks Are Surging, PwC Warns</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
item.description:
WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform.
The optional feature is designed to help users connect with someone on the service through usernames, as opposed to directly sharing their phone numbers. Username reservations will start rolling out starting today,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korea-Linked macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Analysis
item.description: <p>SentinelOne says macOS.Gaslight uses prompt injection to mislead AI-based malware analysis, steal data, and use Telegram for C2.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-macos-gaslight-malware-ai-prompt-injection/">North Korea-Linked macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Analysis</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Factoring RSA Keys with Many Zeros
item.description: <p>Interesting research on a <a href="https://blog.trailofbits.com/2026/06/12/factoring-short-sleeve-rsa-keys-with-polynomials/">new class</a> of weak RSA keys: keys with lots of zeros. It turns out that these keys are out in the wild.</p> <blockquote><p>The badkeys project is an open-source service that checks public keys for known vulnerabilities. While developing this tool, Hanno collected a massive number of real-world keys from public sources, including Certificate Transparency logs, internet-wide TLS and SSH scans, PGP keys, and many others. By searching this dataset for unexpectedly sparse RSA moduli, we uncovered a large number of keys in the wild with the patterns in Figure 1...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier
